Prompt: Create a CareReserve Clinical Operations platform that supports offline-first chronic-care scheduling and follow-up, designed for patients, clinic staff, providers, merchants (e.g., partner labs), and platform operations administrators. Patients authenticate with a local username and password to request and manage reservations, submit supplemental notes and attachments (PDF/JPG/PNG up to 10 MB each, max 5 files per reservation), participate in order-level chat tied to a single reservation, and complete post-transaction two-way reviews after a reservation is marked Complete. Providers and staff confirm, reschedule, cancel, and close reservations; they also configure chronic-disease follow-up plans based on exam-result tags (e.g., A1C banding, blood-pressure stage) with task frequency rules such as “every 14 days” or “every 3 months,” then record structured follow-up outcomes and adherence status. Operations administrators manage role-based access and arbitration of negative-review appeals, and can configure request-approval-allocation workflows for scarce resources (appointment slots, care-team time, devices) with conditional branches, joint approval vs any-one approval, SLA deadlines (default 48 business hours), and offline reminders delivered as in-app notifications. Analytics consumers (operations and clinical quality roles) use reporting capabilities for funnel, retention, and content-quality metrics (completion, engagement, share) plus A/B experiment configuration that assigns cohorts deterministically by user ID hash for reproducible offline experiments.The backend runs as a single Docker-deployable service built with NestJS for modular domain APIs, TypeORM for persistence mapping, and PostgreSQL for local data storage and querying. Interface capabilities are grouped into Authentication/Account APIs (registration, login, password reset via security questions, session issuance, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes), Access Control APIs (RBAC with role, permission, and data-scope policies; all privileged operations require an audit record), Reservation APIs (state machine enforcing Create→Confirm→Reschedule/Cancel→Complete; reschedule cutoff 2 hours before start; cancellation refunds are rule-based: 100% if ≥24 hours, 50% if 2–24 hours, 0% if <2 hours), Follow-up Plan APIs (tag ingestion, plan templates, task schedules, adherence analytics), Communication/Notification APIs (order-level chat with read status, harassment controls via rate limits of 20 messages/minute and a configurable sensitive-word dictionary, plus customer-service ticket escalation), Trust & Rating APIs (reviews allowed within 14 days post-completion; configurable rating dimensions 1–5; appeals open for 7 days with arbitration outcomes recorded; credit tiers computed nightly from last 90 days with anti-fraud flags for same-device or same-IP bursts), and Analytics APIs (event instrumentation for impression/click/read-completion/conversion, exports as CSV files stored locally). Core data models include UUID primary keys, immutable audit trails for state transitions, indexed fields on reservation status/time, user role, and event timestamps, and encrypted-at-rest columns for identity documents with masking on retrieval; idempotency keys are required for create/submit actions to prevent duplicates under retries. Non-functional requirements include offline operation with no external dependencies, p95 API latency under 300 ms for common queries on a single machine, least-privilege access, and tamper-evident audit logs retained for 7 years for healthcare compliance expectations.
